headline: Barron's Picks And Pans: Facebook, Nokia, Regeneron And More
summary:
This weekend's Barron's cover story presents the results from the Barron's 2020 Midyear Roundtable.
	Other featured articles look how to manage risk in a time of market volatility and which companies the U.S. government is and is not bailing out.

headline: Tesla Reduces Model Y Prices; Now Starts Uner $50K
Elon Musk Passes Warren Buffett, Still Behind Bezos As World's Richest Person
summary: Move over, Oracle. There's a bigger billionaire on the block.

headline: The Week In Cannabis: Stocks In Green, FDA's CBD Report, Amazon's AWS, KushCo's Earnings
summary: This week started with some good news out of Amazon.com, Inc. (NASDAQ: AMZN), which included a cannabis company in its AWS Data Exchange, a marketplace for data run by Amazon Web Services, for the first time ever.

headline: Carter Worth And Mike Khouw's Kraft Heinz Trade
summary: CNBC Options Action's Carter Worth and Mike Khouw recommended on June 19 a bullish options trade in Kraft Heinz Co (NASDAQ: KHC). Based on Worth's bullish technical view, Khouw sold the August $32.50 put for $1.90.

headline: Tesla Confirms New Date For Investor, Battery Day
summary: Elon Musk has been teasing Tesla Inc (NASDAQ: TSLA) "battery day" for many months now. After delays due to the COVID-19 pandemic, Musk announced battery day would be combined with investor day.
